events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer with raw JSON. Scenario: residential pool/spa equipotential bonding, rough-complete, missing perimeter bond grid. NEC Art. 680 requires equipotential bonding grid around perimeter in pool/spa area (NEC 680.26(B)(1) perimeter surfaces; 680.26(B)(2) alternative means). Missing = fail, non-compliant/installed-defective, high risk due to shock/drowning. Need produce JSON exactly matching schema. Need fill fields appropriately. Not too much. Need ensure no prose. JSON only.

Let's craft fields:
decision: fail
risk: high
work_stage: rough-complete
component_conditions: ["installed-defective","non-compliant"]
percent_complete: 90 maybe. missing bond grid means almost complete but missing critical component.
value: 0 (not asked numeric reading)
sound_source: "" or null? Schema string default maybe empty. Use "".
confidence: 1.0
event_time: 0.0
rate: 0.0
order: []
workable: []
findings: ["Missing perimeter equipotential bonding grid at pool/spa deck", "No effective means to equalize voltage gradients between conductive pool components, metallic equipment, and surrounding surface"]
actions: ["Install perimeter equipotential bonding grid in accordance with NEC 680.26(B)", "Bond grid to conductive pool shells, metallic parts, fixed metal equipment, and all required components", "Inspect and test continuity before close-out"]
rationale: "NEC Article 680 requires an equipotential bonding grid to reduce voltage gradients in the pool/spa area; its absence creates shock/electrocution hazard and violates 680.26(B)."
references: ["NEC 680.26(B)(1) Equipotential Bonding Grid", "NEC 680.26(B)(2) Alternate Means"]

Should component_conditions include installed-defective? Missing perimeter bond grid means defect because installed electrical bonding system incomplete; non-compliant applies. "installed-defective" is a category that may apply. Good.

Need output raw JSON no backticks. Ensure valid JSON with strings.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Missing perimeter equipotential bonding grid at pool/spa deck",
    "No effective means to equalize voltage gradients between conductive pool components, metallic equipment, and surrounding surfaces"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install perimeter equipotential bonding grid in accordance with NEC 680.26(B)",
    "Bond the grid to conductive pool shells, metallic parts, fixed metal equipment, and all required components",
    "Inspect and test continuity of the equipotential bonding system before close-out"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Article 680 requires an equipotential bonding grid to reduce voltage gradients in the pool/spa area. Its absence creates a shock/electrocution hazard and violates NEC 680.26(B).",
  "references": [
    "NEC 680.26(B)(1) Equipotential Bonding Grid",
    "NEC 680.26(B)(2) Alternate Means"
  ]
}
